channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: Goat Turns Unexpected Guide, Helps Firefighters Find Route To Wildfire In US

media:content.media:description: He later learned on Thursday that the goat's name was Goldie.

item.description: The Rock Creek Canyon fire broke out early on Wednesday in an area west of Cheyenne Mountain State Park.
item.content:encoded: The Rock Creek Canyon fire broke out early on Wednesday in an area west of Cheyenne Mountain State Park.

item.title: "US Bases Our Problem, Not Neighbours": Iranian Experts On Middle East Attacks

media:content.media:description: Iranian experts said that Khamenei's death in the US-Israel strikes has "united the people of Iran".

item.description: Professor Seyed Emamain, Founder of Governance and Policy Thinktank (GPTT), and Ali Salehian, Head of the International Department GPTT, spoke to NDTV about the mass uprising against Ali Khamenei's rule in January to mass support after his death.
item.content:encoded: Professor Seyed Emamain, Founder of Governance and Policy Thinktank (GPTT), and Ali Salehian, Head of the International Department GPTT, spoke to NDTV about the mass uprising against Ali Khamenei's rule in January to mass support after his death.

item.title: Explosions Heard In Iran As Ali Khamenei Is Buried, US Denies Strikes

media:content.media:description: Several areas in Bushehr province were targeted, an official said.

item.description: A US-Israeli projectile reportedly hit a military headquarters on the outskirts of Bushehr, home to Tehran's only civilian nuclear plant, on Thursday.
item.content:encoded: A US-Israeli projectile reportedly hit a military headquarters on the outskirts of Bushehr, home to Tehran's only civilian nuclear plant, on Thursday.

item.title: How Israel Used 'Kill-Plot' Intel To Convince Trump To Attack Iran Again

media:content.media:description: On Thursday, Trump and Netanyahu spoke again

item.description: Tehran, for years, has openly threatened to retaliate against Trump for America's alleged role in the assassination of Qassem Soleimani, who was a top general of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps during the president's first term.
item.content:encoded: Tehran, for years, has openly threatened to retaliate against Trump for America's alleged role in the assassination of Qassem Soleimani, who was a top general of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps during the president's first term.

item.title: Andy Burnham Set To Become Next UK PM As 80% Labour MPs Back Him

media:content.media:description: Burnham said on social media that he was "deeply grateful" for the support of Labour MPs.

item.description: Andy Burnham is on the brink of becoming Britain's next prime minister after securing the backing on Thursday from 80% of Labour lawmakers in a party leadership contest.
item.content:encoded: Andy Burnham is on the brink of becoming Britain's next prime minister after securing the backing on Thursday from 80% of Labour lawmakers in a party leadership contest.

item.title: OpenAI's Number 2 Executive Fidji Simo Steps Down To Focus On health

media:content.media:description: The 40-year-old French-American executive had been on medical leave since April.

item.description: Top Silicon Valley executive Fidji Simo announced Wednesday she is leaving her full-time role at OpenAI to go part-time as an advisor and focus on recovery from a chronic illness.
item.content:encoded: Top Silicon Valley executive Fidji Simo announced Wednesday she is leaving her full-time role at OpenAI to go part-time as an advisor and focus on recovery from a chronic illness.

item.title: Ex-Epoch Times Finance Head Pleads Guilty In $67 Million Fraud

media:content.media:description: Conspiracy charge relating to illegal financial transactions carries prison sentence of up to 10 years.

item.description: Judge Victor Marrero did not immediately set a sentencing date for Weidong "Bill" Guan, 63, who remains free on bail.
item.content:encoded: Judge Victor Marrero did not immediately set a sentencing date for Weidong "Bill" Guan, 63, who remains free on bail.

item.title: Charlie Kirk Killing: Roommate Says Suspect "Wishes He Hadn't Done It"

media:content.media:description: Tyler Robinson faces the death penalty if he is found guilty of shooting Charlie Kirk in last September.

item.description: The man accused of murdering influential right-wing campaigner Charlie Kirk broke down in tears of regret days after the killing, according to video testimony played in a US court on Thursday.
item.content:encoded: The man accused of murdering influential right-wing campaigner Charlie Kirk broke down in tears of regret days after the killing, according to video testimony played in a US court on Thursday.

item.title: Florida Airport Renamed After Trump, He Thanks Palm Beach Voters

media:content.media:description: "This will soon be one of the Greatest and Most Spectacular Airports anywhere in the World!" Trump said.

item.description: The airport renaming was signed into law by Florida's Republican governor, Ron DeSantis.
item.content:encoded: The airport renaming was signed into law by Florida's Republican governor, Ron DeSantis.

item.title: 8 Indicted For Plotting Drone, Sniper Attack On White House UFC Event

media:content.media:description: Donald Trump and UFC CEO Dana White stand on the Octagon at UFC Freedom 250 on South Lawn, White House.

item.description: According to the new indictment, the plot began in May, when the group began amassing money, firearms, ammunition, body armor, explosives, drones, medical equipment, communications equipment and other items.
item.content:encoded: According to the new indictment, the plot began in May, when the group began amassing money, firearms, ammunition, body armor, explosives, drones, medical equipment, communications equipment and other items.

item.title: 12 Dead In Wildfire In Southern Spain

media:content.media:description: Many people were died in a wildfire near Almeria in southern Spain late Thursday.

item.description: The death toll from a wildfire in Almeria in southern Spain rose to 12 after six more victims were found, authorities said Friday.
item.content:encoded: The death toll from a wildfire in Almeria in southern Spain rose to 12 after six more victims were found, authorities said Friday.

item.title: After Fresh US Strikes On Iran, Trump Briefs Netanyahu On "Moves In Gulf"

media:content.media:description: US President Donald Trump briefed Benjamin Netanyahu on Thursday about US operations in the Gulf.

item.description: US President Donald Trump briefed Benjamin Netanyahu on Thursday about US operations in the Gulf, the Israeli Prime Minister's office said, said, after Iran reported fresh American strikes.
item.content:encoded: US President Donald Trump briefed Benjamin Netanyahu on Thursday about US operations in the Gulf, the Israeli Prime Minister's office said, said, after Iran reported fresh American strikes.

item.title: Ali Khamenei Buried 132 Days After US-Israel Strikes Killed Him, Son Mojtaba Absent

media:content.media:description: Ali Khamenei was buried in his home city of Mashhad in eastern Iran on Friday.

item.description: Former Iranian supreme leader Ali Khamenei was buried Friday in his home city of Mashhad, state television reported, following a ceremony at which his son and successor was not seen.
item.content:encoded: Former Iranian supreme leader Ali Khamenei was buried Friday in his home city of Mashhad, state television reported, following a ceremony at which his son and successor was not seen.

item.title: Syria Says ISIS Was Behind Damascus Bombings During Macron's Visit

media:content.media:description: An official with Syria's Internal Security Forces said the Islamic State group was behind the bombings.

item.description: A Syrian security official said Thursday that the cell responsible for two bomb blasts during French President Emmanuel Macron's visit to Damascus this week was affiliated with the Islamic State group.
item.content:encoded: A Syrian security official said Thursday that the cell responsible for two bomb blasts during French President Emmanuel Macron's visit to Damascus this week was affiliated with the Islamic State group.

item.title: Venezuela Earthquake: Death Count Rises To 3,889, Nearly 17,000 Injured

media:content.media:description: The United Nations is meanwhile trying to raise some $300 million in recovery funds for Venezuela.

item.description: The death toll from the powerful twin tremors that struck Venezuela two weeks ago has risen to at least 3,889, a government report published Thursday stated.
item.content:encoded: The death toll from the powerful twin tremors that struck Venezuela two weeks ago has risen to at least 3,889, a government report published Thursday stated.

item.title: North Korea Decides On Measures To Strengthen Its Nuclear Forces: Report

media:content.media:description: Kim Jong Un inspecting weapons at North Korea's National Defence Science Research Institute.

item.description: North Korea has decided on measures to strengthen its nuclear forces "quantitatively and qualitatively" as leader Kim Jong Un called for modernising its military, state media KCNA reported on Friday.
item.content:encoded: North Korea has decided on measures to strengthen its nuclear forces "quantitatively and qualitatively" as leader Kim Jong Un called for modernising its military, state media KCNA reported on Friday.

item.title: US-Iran War Live Updates: Iran Reports Fresh American Strikes, Trump Updates Netanyahu

media:content.media:description: The US also revoked a sanctions waiver for Iranian oil.

item.description: Iran-US War News LIVE: The US military has hit a variety of military sites and port facilities after blaming Iran for targeting several merchant vessels off the coast of Oman, sparking Iranian fire then as well.
item.content:encoded: Iran-US War News LIVE: The US military has hit a variety of military sites and port facilities after blaming Iran for targeting several merchant vessels off the coast of Oman, sparking Iranian fire then as well.

item.title: US Holds Off New Tariffs On Commercial Aircraft Imports

media:content.media:description: Trump's proclamation nonetheless keeps the door open to the possibility of further action down the line.

item.description: The United States has refrained from imposing new tariffs on commercial aircraft and parts after an investigation into the sector, according to a proclamation signed Thursday by President Donald Trump.
item.content:encoded: The United States has refrained from imposing new tariffs on commercial aircraft and parts after an investigation into the sector, according to a proclamation signed Thursday by President Donald Trump.

item.title: Video: Over 900 Snakes Break Free Amid China Floods, Woman Dead, Many Bitten

media:content.media:description: The snakes that escaped from the farms include venomous species, including cobras.

item.description: A woman has reportedly died after being bitten by a snake and reaching the hospital late due to roadblocks and flood in Hengzhou, a city in Guangxi.
item.content:encoded: A woman has reportedly died after being bitten by a snake and reaching the hospital late due to roadblocks and flood in Hengzhou, a city in Guangxi.

item.title: Japanese Woman Arrested For Sewing Housemate's Lips Shut

media:content.media:description: Japan police arrested a woman on suspicion of assault after sewing her housemate's lips shut.

item.description: Woman arrested in Koga, Japan, for allegedly sewing a housemate’s lips shut with needle and thread.
item.content:encoded: Woman arrested in Koga, Japan, for allegedly sewing a housemate’s lips shut with needle and thread.
